title: Fire Cast Upon the Earth
subtitle: Christ longs to be baptized in his own blood — and to set you free from every love that holds you back from him

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the world fall away.
If you have purged yourself of your vices,
you will kindle within yourself the fire of divine love.
Wait here, in silence, for that fire.
gospel: I came to cast fire upon the earth,
and what do I will, but that it be kindled?
But I have a baptism to be baptized with;
and how am I straitened until it be accomplished!
Do not think that I came to send peace upon the earth;
I came not to send peace, but the sword.
I came to separate a man against his father,
and a daughter against her mother,
and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.
He that loveth father, or mother, or son more than me,
behold: Consider the fire he came to cast: not flame, but the fervor of the Holy Spirit,
the love of God and of neighbor, sent into the earth — that is, into human hearts —
to consume sin and make new the soul.
And consider the time of this fire's sending:
it is not accomplished until he himself has suffered.
Behold him say: Before I send the Spirit, I will give myself to be crucified.
Behold the baptism he longs for — the drenching, the dyeing of his own blood in his blessed Passion.
See how he is straitened, anguished, made sorrowful,
until this baptism, and the salvation of men, is brought to its end.
Behold, too, the sword: not war, but the cutting away
of those bonds of carnal affection by which we love one another so much that we serve God less.
Behold a man who, held back by love of his kin,
called them to a feast, and at the table begged the one he trusted most:
if you love me, put your little finger into the fire for my sake —
and that one, fearing the pain, refused, and showed no faith at all.
consider: Nothing so stirs and kindles us to love God as that he suffered for us,
and washed us from our sins in his own blood.
He had nothing in himself to grieve over,
yet he was anguished by our afflictions,
and in the hour of death wore sorrow —
not from fear of his own death, but from the delay of our redemption.
He came not to break the love a man owes his father and mother,
but lest a man set that love before his own salvation, before the worship of God.
For where honor to father or mother cannot be kept without offense to God
and the ruin of one's own salvation,
then hatred toward one's own is piety toward God.
Charity is so ordered that a man must love God above all things, even above himself.
For no love, however great, no friendship, should hold you back
from pursuing the things that belong to the honor of God.
Whatever is loved, must be loved under God and for the sake of God.
And these are the five hindrances those who would follow him must take away.
If you taste perfectly the sweetness of divine love within you,
you will care nothing for the sweetness of the world.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, splendor of the Father's glory,
send into me the fire and fervor of the Holy Spirit,
that the love of God and of neighbor may be kindled, increased, and made perfect in me.
Grant me to leave behind entirely the affection of carnal kinship and the love of bodily delight,
and to love you above all things;
to hold consideration and discretion in all I do,
and never to trust in the security of my own strength or merits;
but always to send to you the embassy of tears and of good works,
and to seek peace from you and bear it away.
Grant me also to renounce all things in affection, and to leave all things in deed,
that I may be your true disciple. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Ignem veni mittere in terram, et quid volo, nisi ut accendatur?
latinJewel.english: I came to cast fire upon the earth, and what do I will, but that it be kindled?
